subject: Physics
title: The Measurement Problem
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Measurement Problem.

hook.text: Quantum mechanics tells us what happens when we measure, but not why measurement is special.

buildup.text: Before measurement, particles exist in superposition—multiple states at once. After, just one.

discovery.text: What causes the transition? The math doesn't explain it. Physicists call this the measurement problem.

twist.text: Different interpretations offer different answers. None has been proven right or wrong experimentally.

climax.text: After a century, this remains quantum mechanics' most embarrassing unsolved question.

punchline.text: The best theory ever. It can't explain its own measurements.

quiz.question: What is the measurement problem in quantum mechanics?
options: Why measurement causes superposition to collapse to one outcome
Why we can't measure very small particles
